collab_recs_with_scores.address: 91, Jalan SS 3/31, Taman Universiti, 47300 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: Yeong Yoong
reviews.text: Family-run budget western food. Basically you order at the stall and pay there. They accept cash only. They'll give you a plastic cup labelled with alphabet. When your order is ready they'll call the alphabet and you've to collect from the stall. The same people are operating the drinks stall. Waiting time was not as long as I thought. Food was actually not bad  You can order satay from the satay stall at the opposite coffeeshop while waiting for your mains. Very nice

If you're hungry af pls don't come
1)price are not match with the food
2)is no aircond so is hot inside
3)the potion are small
4)if you can wait for 1hour+ you're welcome
5)a restaurant just 2 ppl working is ridiculous
6)rm18 for a small potion chicken chop I can have a nice meal set outside
Last time the uncle were still there the food is nice, cheap, big potion and fast
This is my experience inside the restaurant ~no next time

reviews.author: Nancy Ng
reviews.text:
1st visit. Fast serving.

As per rated...
All rating is depending on individual preference 

Disappointed with the taste & quality of food......

Chicken chop without chicken taste & texture.

Owner should look into improving the quality.

Price charge similar as per other restaurant.

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Upstairs Caf
collab_recs_with_scores.address: First Floor, 12A, Jalan SS 12/1b, Ss 12, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: Nur Shamsiah
reviews.text: Love the food here. I really enjoy the taste and plan to come again. The dessert also suit my taste too. some of it got a little bit sweet but it is okay for me.

reviews.author: Farah Isza
reviews.text: It was my first time in here. The entrance is cute. Get the vibe here. The counter is clear with their menu. Easy to choose. Try their Aglio Olio Chicken and kinda like it. Can add chili flakes if you want. They put it on a separate counter. The seats are quite comfy but limited. Came around 730pm after work, and there are a lot of people. Got outdoor sits at the balcony, too.

reviews.author: Blackraven
reviews.text: Hidden upstairs with only a small signage to indicate where the place is. If a place can be famous by word of mouth grapevines, it must be really good. Tried the Beef Lagsana, which was amazing. It came with green basil sauce, a pleasant surprise! Highly recommended. I also read good reviews on their Cabonara, so will try it next time!

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Restoran Oregi
collab_recs_with_scores.address: 53, Jalan SS 15/4b, Ss 15, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: Sheldon Vinsent
reviews.text: One of the OG spots in SS 15. The lamb mandy was delicious and they gave quite a good portion. Would have preferred if they used basmati rice instead of the normal ome though. Also, the restaurant feels very old and even the menu looks dated. In short, the shop could use some sprucing up.

reviews.author: Edmund JP
reviews.text: The Arabian food here was fantastic. The lamb was tender. Absolutely love it. The service was good and friendly too. The taste was absolutely delicious. Parking is a problem in this commercial square, but it is not impossible. Highly recommend the lamb stew and the signature lamb rice (that looks like a burger from the menu photo)

reviews.author: Croco Tai
reviews.text: Good service and tasty food, recommended. If you have a large appetite you can go for their LARGE spaghetti  Yummy

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Teaffani Catering
collab_recs_with_scores.address: 8, Jalan Pelukis U1/46A Kawasan Perindustrian Temasya, Hicom-glenmarie Industrial Park, 40150 Shah Alam, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: Siti Noor Intanbaizura Mohamad Aripin
reviews.text: We had a really good experience with Teaffani Catering during our 4 days meeting. The food was delicious, well-presented, and consistently good throughout. I coordinated with Ms. Eva for the order and food arrangements - she was so pleasant and easy to work with. The waitstaff were also very helpful and supportive during our meeting days.

reviews.author: Salmi Bawasa
reviews.text: Teaffani is halal certified. The employees are also mostly Muslims. The presentation is beautiful. The food is good with very generous portion. The food package is also budget friendly. They have many categories to choose from. I chose the lowest category and yet it is wholesome. Highly recommended whether it is for internal event at the office or external events. Teaffani knows what they should bring. They even spare a table just incased the external event does not have enough tables. Not much of a fuss before the event. I am very happy with the service definitely

reviews.author: MHC Medical Network
reviews.text: My office catered lunch buffet from Teaffani for the year end Christmas party. Food was delicious and the quantity was just the right amount. The tables were nicely set up with Christmas deco. The helpers were very helpful always ensuring that food and drinks are timely replenished and for cleaning up after the party. Everyone enjoyed the wide spread especially the grilled lamb, mushroom soup and bread and butter pudding. It would have been nice if the rustic toast bread was more to accompany the flavourful mushroom soup. Overall I am happy to have catered from them and would definitely recommend them to friends.

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Serai @Paradigm Mall
collab_recs_with_scores.address: Lot GB-15 & 15A, Ground Floor Boulevard, Paradigm Mall, 1, Jalan SS 7/26a, Ss 7, 47301 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: Simranjeet Kaur
reviews.text:
We went to Serai for a Raya lunch celebration with the office, and it was a great experience overall! The food was delicious with generous portions-definitely worth the price. I had the carbonara, and it was creamy and full of flavor. They also offer vegetarian options, which is a thoughtful touch for those with dietary preferences.

The staff were friendly and helpful throughout our meal, making sure we were well taken care of.

The only downside was the noise level-it got pretty loud, almost like a market, so its not the best place if youre looking for a quiet lunch. But if you dont mind the lively atmosphere, the food and service definitely make up for it.

Would come back again for the tasty dishes!

reviews.author: hanila suriani
reviews.text: Had a great experience dining at Serai. Mamun served me and he did a great job - he checked in to make sure everything was okay and if I needed anything else. The food was delicious and reasonably priced. Will definitely come again!

reviews.author: Jess Lee
reviews.text: The serai platter is a good choice if you want to sample a bit of everything.  Generous portion. Steamed squid with lime was alright for me, perhaps the lime taste wast slightly too strong. I personally like the mee mamak goreng.

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Thai Thai, Sunway Pyramid
collab_recs_with_scores.address: Jalan PJS 11/15, Bandar Sunway, 47500 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: Winnie Tan
reviews.text: Compared to the first time I ate here, the quality of the food has definitely dropped a lot. However, it has improved quite a bit since the post-COVID period. This time, since there were two of us dining, we ordered  la carte. The Tom Yum noodles were spicy and sour enough, and the basil chicken rice had a good flavor. As for the dessert, it was a bit too sweet. I hope there will be a pleasant surprise when I visit again next time.

reviews.author: Kangwah lee
reviews.text: Thai Thai is a great Thai restaurant with a lovely Thai-inspired atmosphere. We tried the classic Tom Yum soup, which was full of tangy and spicy flavors, and the seafood was really fresh. The Pad Thai was also delicious, with a perfect balance of sweet and mildly spicy flavors. The service was excellent, with friendly and efficient staff. Overall, its a fantastic spot for dining with friends or family. If you're a fan of Thai food, I highly recommend it.

reviews.author: Jalitha Vishika Vithanage (Vish)
reviews.text:
This thai restaurant has a dedicated vegetarian menu which seems to be mostly vegan.

The dishes overall were decent for a non vegan place, flavorful and fresh, quality ingredients. The papaya salad and tom yum was good

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Rasamas
collab_recs_with_scores.address: F4.41, Jalan SS 16/1, Ss 16, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Chili's Empire Subang
collab_recs_with_scores.address: G-17, Ground Floor Empire Shopping Gallery, Jalan SS 16/1, Ss 16, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia
collab_recs_with_scores.editorial_summary: Family-friendly chain serving classic Tex-Mex & American fare in a Southwestern-style setting

reviews.author: Ravindran Raghavan
reviews.text:
Service was excellent. Habib served us attentively. Ambiance was superb.
Scampi pasta was fine and prawns were fresh.
Premium wine by glass had a tinge of oxidation, maybe turnover is slow?

reviews.author: Fyqa Yunus
reviews.text:
Food: the food is not consistent in term of texture. Been visiting here twice within a week and ordered the same chips and salsa but received different texture of salsa. The first time was thick and good but the recent one was too watery.

Service: The staff was very attentive. Fast response to any request.

Sadly some of the menu such as pasta with meatball is no longer in the menu.

reviews.author: Ahmad Kamarul Zainal Abidin (Kamarul Zainal)
reviews.text: It seems that my experience here was quite mixed. The food was average but still acceptable, with some portions being large enough for two people. However, the service was very slow, and some dishes were missed among our group. They might need to improve their time management and ensure that all dishes are delivered correctly.

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Kinpachi Japanese Restaurant
collab_recs_with_scores.address: 1.06, Wisma Dicor, Jalan SS 17/1A, Ss 17, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: WH Teh
reviews.text:
This is a Japanese restaurant that has been in business for many years and still surviving, shall have quite a number of loyal customers I believe.
We came for a dinner in weekend.
The place is quite crowded and we end up sharing long table with another customers in a small room.
The ambient at common area is cozy, bit not the privacy room
Food serves with reasonable waiting time.
Taste is acceptable , but is not the best in Subang areas though.
Worth trying and suggest to book earlier of weekend.
Parking is plenty, and need to climb upstairs.
Overall, is good experience to have a Japanese dinner here.

reviews.author: Dx 7
reviews.text:
Nice place. The food is really good. Price abit on the higher side but worth it. Recommend to try their grilled food !!

I came here once during lunch, but the food didn't feel its on par as dinner time. Suspect the chef is different for lunch and dinner. Dine here for dinner, will not disappoint !

reviews.author: dennis woo
reviews.text: Waited over an hour. Sorry the food here doesn't meet my standard. Just look at how bad my Japanese Egg Omelette Roll looked. The green tea no flavour. Rice also not nice. Wasabi not fresh. Cawan mushi not smooth.

collab_recs_with_scores.name: The Chicken Rice Shop Subang Parade
collab_recs_with_scores.address: Lot LG 10G (1), Lower Ground Floor, 5, Jalan SS16/1, Ss 16, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: Yi San Wong
reviews.text: Ordered 2 of the same thing, but chicken portion is so different (refer to photo, it was served like that). Chicken is dry.

reviews.author: Maya Ayumie
reviews.text: My fav nasi ayam so far. Never make me upset (Roasted chicken rice). Their menu's taste consistent which is delicious for my tummy ;) Their service fast but sometimes a little bit slow, especially during peak hour (lunch and dinner time) Should try their 'rojak buah' ,  recommended.  Nice!

reviews.author: Prasil Lakshmanan
reviews.text: Tried their Nyonya Pie Tee on two separate days. Don't recall this dish looking like this from other outlets, at least few months ago I tried. There's a lot less fillings and more fried onions than anything else. Even the fried onions looks too different from the soft, light golden-brown onions you see in their official photos.

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Texas Chicken Sunway Pyramid
collab_recs_with_scores.address: Sunway Pyramid Mall Unit No. LG2.127A & OB3.LG2.1D, Lower Ground, Two and Oasis Boulevard, Bandar Sunway, 47500 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia
collab_recs_with_scores.editorial_summary: Fast-food chain offering fried chicken, sandwiches, wings & Southern-influenced sides.

reviews.author: Ilamaran Ramamoorthy
reviews.text:
It was my first time dining at this outlet. I have eaten at other Texas Chicken outlets before.

I tried one of the Menu Rahmah dishes for a late lunch. The rice portion was quite small compared to the very first time I had the same meal at another place. Should be genuine in serving the budget meal. Otherwise, don't serve it. Hope the management will look into this.

Otherwise, it's a nice and big store. Staff are courteous and friendly.

reviews.author: Kick K (Zakk Fazz)
reviews.text: The Limau Berapi Chicken Tenders were absolutely delicious. The meat was incredibly juicy, coated in a flavorful blend of sweet and sour calamansi with a fiery spiciness. Its like sweet and sour chicken but with a kick! The balance of tangy citrus and spice made each bite satisfying. I had their fried chicken as well and the portion sizes were generous.

reviews.author: Edwin Yin
reviews.text: Food is serve quick despite a lot of crowd, and they are piping hot. I tried their garlic chicken, not really nice, still prefer the ori taste. Their mash potato taste quite weird. But overall still better than KFC.

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Golfers Terrace
collab_recs_with_scores.address: Saujana Golf & Country Resort, Jalan Subang, Seksyen, U2 Shah Alam, 40150 Shah Alam, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: Lucas Law
reviews.text:
A nice place to have a tea break or lunch with a nice view of the golf course.

Prices was okay. We had their Teh Tarik for RM7.50 each and it was actually good. The French fries were good too.

The servers were very nice and accomodating as well. Overall, its nice.

reviews.author: Jon Whittington
reviews.text: I was lucky to get an invite to play 18 holes golf at Saujana GCC. Arriving early I dropped by the Terrace for coffee to wait. Service is good and efficient. The whole experience was a treat for me so I did not grumble about the expensive cafe latte. The menu seems extensive and I am sure members' prices are discounted. The seating is spacious and comfortable and if enjoying a premier golf experience at Saujana then the Golfers Terrace fits well into the day.

reviews.author: Tan Kok Kee
reviews.text: Nice place to have lunch with a nice view of the golf course. Delicious

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Paradise Dynasty @ Paradigm Mall
collab_recs_with_scores.address: Lot 1F-15, Paradigm Mall, 1, Jalan SS 7/26a, SS7, 47301 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: MsOmNom
reviews.text:
Had a pleasant meal at Paradise Dynasty in Paradigm Mall recently. We arrived early at around 11.45am on a weekday so no crowds at that time. The standout for me was definitely their limited-time yellow wine xiao long bao - rich, flavorful, and something quite different from the usual offerings. The wonton soup also deserves a mention with its tasty broth, while the century egg with chili oil was nicely done and balanced.

On the other hand, the fried noodles and stir-fried cabbage were a bit too oily for my liking, which made those dishes less enjoyable.

The restaurant itself is nicely done up with a clean, modern interior. Service was generally good and attentive, though the staff did feel a little bit too eager when taking orders - we had to ask for more time before placing everything.

Overall though, it was a good experience and Id happily come back again to try more of their xiao long bao selections.

reviews.author: Eddie Lim
reviews.text:
Food was as expected. I think the fried rice here is more oily compared to other branch.
However I want to comment about the wooden panel beside the seat. I know this restaurant is dated but you need to clean it up. Is full of dust and is at eye level that I can't ignored the whole dinner session.
The Buddha could be decoration but everytime I eat here I feel guilty indulging meat in front of Buddha.

reviews.author: Joshua Anthony Hajok
reviews.text:
3 dishes.

La mian pork belly : 4.5/5
This noodle- wrapped in saucy lard. Add the chilli they have at this place- kaboom . Can eat without.

Pork belly bun: 4/5
Simple and straightforward.

La mian Prawn dumpling in hot chilli vinaigrette: 4.5/5- wished it was just a little bit more savoury from the mala. Almossssssst.

Overall. 5 star.  Because nice.

The atmosphere/interior design of this place is amazing.

Not great for my wallet. Really. Sigh. But overall good.

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Suki-Ya Paradigm Mall
collab_recs_with_scores.address: Lot UG-13 UG Floor, 1, Jalan SS 7/26a, SS7, 47301 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: Janis Gmail
reviews.text:
We don't expect your quality to be like Ippudo ramen kind of quality. But to pay Rm15 for a bowl of ramen, it's not extremely cheap as well. The quality is even worst than kopitiam.

Sukiya a company with so many outlets and you are so proud to advertise your brand of the year logo and award, but you have neglected the most basic food quality. I'm not sure how your food cooking SOP looks like, but to serve this kind of Ramen (in Lump), make me so speechless.

reviews.author: Local Guardsman
reviews.text:
I mean you cant really go wrong with all-you-can-eat buffets. People were gulping the meats but personally I mostly filled my tummy with the sushis :v

Free flow ice cream was also a plus.

reviews.author: 4nsfr
reviews.text:
I visit this restaurant occasionally because Im not a big fan of buffets, as I get full easily. Its a great place to enjoy a meal, though!

Typically, theres a long queue after 6pm so its better to go early. Fortunately, our office is nearby, so I dont have to wait in line. You could also try going in the middle of the month when it might be less crowded.

Overall, it offers good food at a good value.

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Restoran Sari Ratu Prima Kelana Jaya
collab_recs_with_scores.address: AG 10 & AG 11, Pusat Bandar Kelana Jaya, Jalan SS 6/5a, Dataran Glomac, 47301 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: abil
reviews.text: 2nd time makan sini. The food is not up to par with the quality, flavour, portion and the price. Genuinely disgusted when i found a few hairs in my food. One was long, the others are in the chicken breadcrumbs. disguting. The place is unhygienic and someone reviewed they found a cooked cockroach in their dish. Disgusting, KKM please look into this restaurant. Also, the staff be looking you up and down when you genuinely dont know how to order because apparently someone needs to assist you ? weird. Do better

reviews.author: Pam Ko Fit Foodie
reviews.text:
My English Korean family loves padang style food so much. So today after being a regular to sederhana masakan, we wanted to try sari ratu prima. We arrived around 1 PM, which considered not an ideal lunch time for enjoying freshly cook piping hot dishes, though we decided to go since we woke up late.

The dishes were mostly gone and some left after gathered into pile of small plates once they transferred to from the rectangular tray.
We chose our all time padang dishes like dengdeng belado, rendang daging, daun ubi(singkong?), chengkol, sambal petai udang, nanka gulai with 2 rice.

But the rice seems expensive and very small in portion, so we had asked one extra. But I saw some other customers were having a massive portion of individual plates even though it's not nasi campur setting!

Anyway we also orders 2 ice water and one coffee O kosong panas for me as I needed some caffeine to wake myself fully up.
The dishes we chose were all delicious and lovely, but due to the fact we went quite later lunch time, the food wasn't that warm which is one downside,  but we won't repeat to come late next time anyway.

Lets talk about some our favorite padang dishes! Here, the dengdeng belado topped with red chiili is just ok, thanks to freshly made red sambal, but the beef itself wasn't that amazing unlike that of Sederhana we enjoyed. It is bcoz the beef here was way way too thin and deep oil fried I can tell, the texture was like some sort of chips in a hint of meat form. It should be more chewy and meatier jerky! Here it's just flaky chips or keropok like texture in oily form.
Please better improve the thickness of the dengdeng, should be more thicker so that customers can savor the beefy flavors while biting and chewing, not feathery ultra light crisps! 

The staff who was explaining the price one by one was very friendly and attentive, except the one upsetting price dispute for sambal prawn petai upon paying the bill lastly.
That said, undeniably its the most surprising cons to us after the meal, that the price we were told was rm8.50 for sambal udang petai when I asked pointing it with my hand last minute, so I said satu pinggan. But it turned out to be rm 16.25, almost double price just for 5 prawns. It's ok 5 prawns for rm8.50,  not for rm16.25. Basically the each prawn costs over rm3 each which seemed very very expensive. Since I live here, when I can buy fresh prawns per kilo for rm 40 easily, and i usually get 20 fresh prawns for just half kilo(rm20)
So i told that staff who also was in charge of the payment counter, but he said it's the price, but they only gave us 5 prawns for such high price? If we knew, we could have ordered another plate for daging rendang something.
Other than these two dishes. All is amazing and tasty! 

Just white rice potion seems too little for rm2.25, which I couldn't get rid of in my mind until the next meal! , The totall bill after 6% tax added came around rm70.

Interior is simple, yet clean and morden with a nice minang traditional house painting on the wall! Strong aircon and clean washroom too!

Despite of the price dispute for the sambal prawn udang petai, we likely come back soon to try more other yummy looking dishes at the very right time!

reviews.author: tia idrus
reviews.text:
We used to frequent this place when they first opened here. Glad to see that it's still operating and still has many patrons. Food is still good but the selection is the same. If you crave for nasi padang style dishes, this is the place to be.

We were satisfied with the food we had, we ordered our usual selection of dishes: Ikan Nila Terbang, Ayam Goreng, Ikan Keli, sambal ijo and many others. Our favorite is the Ikan Nila terbang which is served together with the black sauce, so good especially if they fry it fresh for you.

Price is on the higher side, definitely more expensive than your regular economy rice but pretty standard for nasi padang dishes. We ordered 8 types of lauk with 4 plates of rice and they set us back about RM200. Of course this varies according to the types of lauk you choose.

Recommended if you're looking for Indonesian-style rice and if you're hungry because most of the lauk are already ready so they'll serve you promptly. Only thing I don't like here is the parking, however, the restaurant does have limited reserved spots for patrons for their convenience.

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Kafe Bawang Merah
collab_recs_with_scores.address: 12A, Jalan SS 12/1b, Ss 12, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: M Low
reviews.text:
When waiting for the SJMC tests to come out, it's either here or the mamak next door.
5/2/25 Mee goreng. Omg, too spicy  Should have a warning sign....is this noodle goreng with chili padi? Even considered spicy for a healthy person and totally unbearable for someone feeling unwell. RM3.50

Kuih is the favourite. Small but delicious. RM1.30 per piece. kuih kaswi, gula hangus, Seri muka and cucur udang.

reviews.author: Shiro Kuro
reviews.text: The food and service is average. But the employee is friendly and gives a smile to most of the customers. The place is clean and cozy, a bit tight on some space. There was an outdoor and indoor space to your choosing. However, the food is a mix of good and bad (Roasted chicken was dry, but Ayam masak lemak cili padi is great).  The kuih is great and delicious. The price is reasonable and it is a great place for a drink and talk.

reviews.author: Peggy Chow
reviews.text:
The food are good, i took the beef rendang and it didnt disappoint. The beef are a bit chewy, and the curry is flavourful.
The cokodok is nice too, crispy on the outside, soft on the inside.

However the place is quite small and itll have long q during lunch hour, the parking is nightmare too.

collab_recs_with_scores.name: The Manhattan Fish Market @ Subang Parade
collab_recs_with_scores.address: Malaysia, Selangor, Subang Jaya, Ss 16, Jalan Kemajuan Subang, LG10E.F.G: 47500

reviews.author: M Low
reviews.text:
Promotion looks amazing until....the dishes arrive and you realize why it's so cheap. The amount is very small. It's ok if you are a small eater and if you want a comfortable quiet place to sit down in Subang Parade after lunch hours.

Promo Monday to Friday 11-6pm for certain dishes.

Fish and chips RM16.90
Alaskan pollack was as big as a hand and lots of crunchy batter. It was nice, but very oily and tiny. Sauce was nice, but so little not enough to dip the fish and all the fries.

RM3.90 add on Mushroom soup ..was tiny. But 100x better than the one in Pizza Hut.

RM9.90 alio oglio, small amount 
Also 100 x better than the pizza hut one. Al dente, crunchy peppers, salty enough, lots of oil, but at least tasty.

Their drinks are expensive, starting RM8.90 ? Cheapest is a bottle of Mineral water for RM3.50? They serve with glasses and ice if you require.

The local Malaysian waiter was very nice, tq.

reviews.author: QQQueen
reviews.text: There has been awhile since my last visit to the Manhattan Fish Market. The Fish and Chips is ok, the tartar sauce is yummy. The Grilled Chicken taste good but the marinated sauce is not apply evenly on the chicken thus some part is too salty.

reviews.author: Aurora
reviews.text: I had the spicy fish & chips. Wished there was more tartar sauce. Been a long time since Ive had manhattan fish market. You can get a free soup if you register (free) for their member. The place is spacious.

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Shihlin Taiwan Street Snacks @ Sunway Pyramid
collab_recs_with_scores.address: Lot F1.AV.128, Sunway Pyramid, No. 3, Jalan PJS 11/15, Bandar Sunway, 46150 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: Tan Grace
reviews.text: 10pm closing but 9.29 already not taking any order & cleaned up all the things. Please train ur staff to be more responsible.

reviews.author: Sherlynn Tan
reviews.text: The chicken is so thin, totally different thickness with branch in jb. No next time.

reviews.author: Marcus Kow
reviews.text:
Id always been a fan of Shihlin food for the past 7 years of my life however the amount of price increases is shocking. Well this isnt really a franchise control but Im just putting out there.

Food is great and quality and portion have never dropped ever since I first ate it and I love their new flavours each time. I still remember their golden boneless chicken, (please bring it back).

I would have nothing to change but if I was picky it would be the cleanliness of the seating area. I understand they are understaff sometimes so please HR hire more staff during peak days and hours

collab_recs_with_scores.name: TheFOODtree Restaurant, Glenmarie
collab_recs_with_scores.address: No.10, Section U1, Jalan Pelukis U1/46, Kawasan Perindustrian Temasya, 40150 Shah Alam, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: Veronica
reviews.text:
We came here for birthday celebration.. primarily choosing this place for the dinosaurs decoration.. the environment is really nice with playground nearby. Super kids friendly..
Good food fast serving, very tempting and delicious.. personally like the crispy fried chicken most..
And most importantly great staff service.. entertaining our requests promptly and friendly.. even offer us happy birthday Dino backdrop
A great memory with family gathering the whole afternoon time spent lovely here~

reviews.author: Hop Poppies Run
reviews.text: Impressive dinosaur displays, playground was sufficient to keep my 5yo twin boys interested for hours. At the restaurant, colouring paper and pencils were given as well. Food taste and quality was ordinary and as expected..nothing to compliment or complain about. It was fully run by foreign men. The playground was a distance from the restaurant, as can be seen in the photos. That side was hot as the power was not turned on. Can come again for kids to play and adults catch up with friends.

reviews.author: Joyce Ang
reviews.text: very nice food , drink can order the jumbo one: refreshing and nice. Fish and chips crispy. Nasi kerabu ayam rendang the sambal is the best. Mac and cheese suitable for kids. And also salmon fried rice delicious (u may request the sambal and add in,  yummy

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Sri Melur Jaya Restaurant
collab_recs_with_scores.address: 26, Jln SS19/6, Ss 19, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: M Low
reviews.text:
25/3/25
Chicken curry is the best. So is the potato dish served with capatti. Default served  fish curry which is sour. You can buy the potato dish for RM1.50, delish.

Feb 2025. Food is ok. Service can be brusque depending on who serves.

Haven't been back here for 2 years and am delighted that it's now so clean and breezy. New addition is the station to grill your ikan bulus, cauliflower Gobi, chicken, etc. ikan bulus was extremely flavourful aka salty and went very well with plain rice! 3 little ones for RM6, 1 plate Gobi for RM5. A piece of fried chicken is RM5,not marinated but still juicy and tender ( not dried out and fried until hard like other mamak chicken)

reviews.author: AlanO
reviews.text:
Posted 4th June 2025
The roti canai is good, fluffy inside bit crispy outside, but a bit on the sweet side. However, the dhall was below average. The tea was ok. Overall tidy and clean, non air condition environment. Fast and efficient service when this took place early in the morning week day.  Price is reasonable. Public parking available.

reviews.author: Explore Nonstop
reviews.text:
One of my go to roti place. The roti canai was fluffy and good flavor, the dhal (yellow) was thick with hints garlic (maybe?) anyway just nice!!
Love the sweet and spicy sambal too, teh tarik is strong

collab_recs_with_scores.name: JM Bariani House Subang Jaya
collab_recs_with_scores.address: 29, Jalan SS 18/6, Ss 18, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: John Loi
reviews.text:
Nasi Beriyani Gam Kambing, and it's a must-order every time we crave flavorful .

The meal portions are just right for one person, making it a satisfying and enjoyable dining choice. Highly recommended for biryani enthusiasts!

reviews.author: Coby Khor
reviews.text:
I ordered the Ayam Berempah Nasi Briyani. The rice was flavorful, but the Ayam Berempah could be better. The service pace was fast; we got our food served within 10 minutes

The only downside is that the parking space here is limited. You need to spend some time finding parking in this area.

reviews.author: Bryan Lee
reviews.text:
Its been 4 years since I gave a review for this place and I thought of why not update since its been so long. And I did.

4 years since I reviewed this place and much have changed. The atmosphere for one is much improved, looks a lot more classy than before as reflective of the kind of restaurant it is, a classy and delicious Malay restaurant that serve above average quality food with above average price.

Speaking about price, it has increased way more than I anticipated! A classic Nasi Lemak Ayam Berempah is already above RM20, and its not close to Village Park Nasi Lemak. But even though there have been a couple changes for better or for worse, what doesnt change is how great and delicious their food is. Their Nasi Briyanj is still one of the best in Subang, and they still have one of the best Mee Bandung! Even my relatives from Muar were commending it to be better than its original recipe from Muar. But the price has also increased together with service charge & sst, one Mee Bandung cost RM20! 

Would I come regularly to pay this restaurant a visit? Certainly not, especially with that pricing. Once awhile still fine, to satisfy my desire of having a good Nasi Briyanj. But will definitely still recommend friends & family to this place & give a try cuz why not?!

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Kafe Tok Ali
collab_recs_with_scores.address: B - 3A - G, Taragon Kelana Jaya, 3, Jalan SS 6/6, Ss 6, 47301 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: Afif Shamim
reviews.text:
Tucked in a cozy corner of Petaling Jaya, Kafe Tok Ali delivers a heartwarming mix of traditional Malay dishes with a modern kopitiam vibe. Whether youre stopping by for breakfast, lunch, or dinner, this spot is a local favourite for its rich flavours, friendly service, and affordable pricing.

What I Loved:
 Signature Nasi Berlauk - Tried the Nasi Daging Bakar and Sambal Hijau - flavorful, spicy, and tender, just like how Tok used to make it!
 Beautifully Plated Meals - Dishes are vibrant and photogenic-perfect for foodies who love to share their makan moments.
 Relaxing Ambience - Air-conditioned space with clean wooden tables, warm lighting, and soft nasyid or classic Malay tunes playing in the background.
 Quick Service - Orders came fast, and the team was attentive and courteous.
 Instagrammable Details - From their retro signage to food plating and rustic table settings, its made for great photos and casual TikToks.

Recommended Dishes:
	Nasi Daging Bakar Tok Ali
	Lontong with Kuah Lodeh
	Teh Tarik Kaw & Pandan Gula Melaka Drinks
	Kuih Melayu for tea-time!

reviews.author: Stephen ong
reviews.text: Fragrant kerabu rice is good on its own. The coconut kerisik could be better. Both chicken and catfish curries did not  elevate the meal. A traditional Terengganu cookup to serve a spicy alternative.

reviews.author: Suka Sempoi
reviews.text:
I give 5 stars for the service.
the food is not to my taste.
the shop was sweltering.  good if there is an exhaust fan or blower.

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Canai 15 Cafe (SS15)
collab_recs_with_scores.address: 2, Jalan SS 15/4, Ss 15, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: Colin Thong
reviews.text: Visited for late breakfast. One of your cleaner, halal 'mamak' restaurants. Corner lot which catches a good breeze. Had a maggi goreng - not as spicy and salty as you would normally - taugeh, sawi and carrots over cabbage for a change but missing the diced taufu cubes, imho. Very interestingly priced, i.e. ramah-like, set lunches. Ironically, my simple meal cost much higher, which made me enquire to which i received no response. Oh well...

reviews.author: AISYY MOHD
reviews.text: I got my lunch to go from Canai SS15. Now they are selling chicken rice! It comes with a good portion, a good price, and a drink as part of the set!  The roasted chicken tastes so good! It's a quick lunch to grab if you're in the SS15 area!

reviews.author: Sunny Sin Chakrabarty
reviews.text: Honestly, it's pretty hard to find a flaw here. The food is delicious always, well seasoned and a variety of options. I've had desi cuisine here that I had never tasted before. The staff are so friendly and kind, ensuring you have a wonderful experience. The decor is so cozy and whoever is in charge of the playlist that plays when I'm there, needs a promotion! The music accompanies your taste buds!

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Loong Kee
collab_recs_with_scores.address: LG2.72, 3, Jalan PJS 11/15, Bandar Sunway, 46150 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: W L
reviews.text: Bought 2 cans of their seaweed pork floss at RM14.90 each, ended up throwing away both cans as it has a plastic smell and taste. Totally inedible. A total waste of money.

reviews.author: SHC
reviews.text:
Many people queue up to buy at their store during Saturday. CNY is coming soon. This is our must eat CNY food.
We ate with Hainam Bread is so delicious !

reviews.author: M Prisheela
reviews.text:
**Review:**

I recently visited this shop to buy chicken coin and had an extremely disappointing experience. I paid RM 12.25 using my debit card, and I received an SMS confirmation of the transaction. Additionally, the charge was reflected in my bank account. Despite having proof of payment, the staff insisted that I pay again, claiming they did not receive the payment.

When I refused to pay a second time, the situation escalated quickly. The staff member became visibly angry and started throwing the debit machine around, creating a very hostile and uncomfortable environment. Their behavior was completely unprofessional and showed a total lack of respect for customers.

I was shocked by the poor service and the aggressive reaction of the staff. This kind of treatment is unacceptable, and I will definitely not be returning to this shop. I strongly advise others to avoid this place due to their terrible customer service and disrespectful staff.

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Wong Kok Char Chan Teng
collab_recs_with_scores.address: F1.55, 3, Jalan PJS 11/15, Bandar Sunway, 47500 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: Veeraphandian Jagaveran
reviews.text:
Visited Wong Kok recently with high expectations, especially since the ambience was comfortable and the service was generally polite. The menu offers a wide variety of Hong Kong-style dishes, and the environment is suitable for casual dining.

However, I couldnt help but feel that some of the food lacked freshness. A few items, particularly the breakfast meats, tasted like they were pre-cooked or frozen, then simply reheated. The texture and flavor just didnt deliver the freshness or depth expected from a dine-in restaurant.

That said, not all dishes were disappointing - some items were tasty and served warm with good presentation. Theres definitely potential, but consistency and food quality need improvement if Wong Kok wants to stand out.

reviews.author: Jackie
reviews.text:
WongKok used to have a branch in Johor Bahru, but as with all businesses who tried expanding southwards, there's this thing about unpredictability of patrons in Johor state, particularly if you are targeting Singaporeans. One moment they "flood" an outlet, the next few months, boredom sets in and they frequent the place lesser and lesser. This happened with Jordan HongKong restaurant and KimGary, they are both struggling to survive monthly.
Compared to Jordan and KimGary,
1. The prices are slightly more affordable.
2. The portions are generous
3. The taste is more delectable and memorable.
We ordered the spaghetti with Cheese and a Pork Chop Western serve. Both came out outstanding! Better than the rest. Their Menu is not extensive, which is fine because better to concentrate on what you are good at rather thsn trying to serve everyone and everything at the same time!

reviews.author: GB Kee
reviews.text: When I bit into the fries, there was a strong taste of old oil, which made it quite unpleasant. The pork chop didnt seem fresh either, and the black pepper sauce was unfortunately one of the worst Ive tasted. Ive honestly never had such a disappointing meal. To the owner - investing money alone isnt enough to run a successful business. It might be worth taking some time to observe how your staff are managing the kitchen and food quality.

collab_recs_with_scores.name: KFC AEON Big Subang
collab_recs_with_scores.address: G02 Floor, AEON BiG Subang, 3, Jalan SS 16/1, Ss 16, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia
collab_recs_with_scores.editorial_summary: Restaurant chain known for its buckets of fried chicken, plus combo meals & sides.

reviews.author: Jo Eats
reviews.text: Only ordered potato wedges. Wedges is quite tasteless. Staff is not super friendly, but acceptable helpful. This branch has alot of take outs.

reviews.author: Sanjivan Raja Segaran
reviews.text: dropped in right before opening. i had the new burger and it was cooked to order. loaded bowl was fresh too. no nonsense kfc experience this time. service was good.

reviews.author: Coby Khor
reviews.text:
First-time visit to this KFC outlet.

Initially, I assumed all KFC branches would maintain consistent food quality, but to my surprise, it fell short of expectations.

I ordered this set and shared with my friend, both of us couldnt finish it due to the unsatisfactory taste.  The chicken tasted bland and weird. However, the cheesy wedges were better.

However, the staff provided good service, and the restaurant was clean.

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Nando's Subang Parade
collab_recs_with_scores.address: Jalan Kemajuan Subang, Ss 16, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia
collab_recs_with_scores.editorial_summary: Afro-Portuguese chain restaurant serving flame-grilled chicken in spicy chilli sauce.

reviews.author: Sharon Ting
reviews.text: Ordered the fully loaded chips without spring onion, the size is not what I expect to get with the price RM18.90. It looks like a side instead of a snack. And the kept on forgot about our order, we have to ask the staff twice to get all our food.

reviews.author: Vasini Aru
reviews.text: Very nice staff, friendly, atmosphere so good, one of the best nando branches i have seen. Met staff named Hafiz.. very friendly, helped us to make the order. Good job!

reviews.author: Dyana Azriel
reviews.text: staff is attentive and friendly. service is amazing. outlet is clean. we had lunch here during mother's day and they surprise my mom with a muffin and rose. deff one of the best nandos outlet and going to be my family's go to!

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Dave's Deli Subang Parade
collab_recs_with_scores.address: Lot G24C, Ground Floor, Subang Parade Shopping Centre, Ss 16, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: Jeff JW
reviews.text:
An iconic restaurant that still retains the quality after many years. Their signature quarter toast chicken is a must try with their in-house gravy.

Their fish n' chips is also really good and quite possibly one of the best I have tasted. The chips as well with their tartare sauce.

reviews.author: Husna Sabri
reviews.text: Ordered salted egg chickent but literally taste like masak lemak?!! The texture is not pleasant its acceptable if the taste it there but its not.. doesnt taste the SALTED EGG AT ALLL.. if i can give zero star then i will.. usually ate ioi city mall.. everytime went there all the dishes taste perfectly good but here first time come already have bad experience..  two on left is from this branch.. other one is from ioi city mall can compare.. the presentation already out!!! Dislike dislike dislike

reviews.author: Timothy John Jindi
reviews.text: Dined at this legendary restaurant on 18.12.24. Had two pies & a can of Coke (RM53.90 total). Highly satisfied with the meal. Worth every cent I paid. Always loved their pies, ever since I first ate at Dave's Deli @ Jaya in the early 90s.

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Uncle Lim's Cafe @Subang Parade
collab_recs_with_scores.address: Subang Parade, Jalan Kemajuan Subang, Ss 16, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: Lau Guan Zhen (Sean)
reviews.text: Nice spot for some good old Malaysian comfort food. Uncle Lims has that nostalgic kopitiam vibe - simple, no fuss, and satisfying. Had the nasi lemak with fried chicken, and it hit the spot. Kaya toast and half-boiled eggs were classic too, just like how it should be. Service was decent, and the place is clean and cozy, great for a chill meal or tea time catch-up. Will swing by again when Im in the area!

reviews.author: Jake Chin
reviews.text:
Came here for the nasi lemak ayam goreng. The waiting time is really long (I suspect that they fry it per order unlike KFC). The chicken is really fresh, the skin is crispy. It tastes really good, but just that the waiting is slightly longer than other main dishes.

The environment is alright, its a little busy around here. The staff is alright too.

Overall, a pretty great place to dine in.

reviews.author: daezjn
reviews.text:
Nestled in old Subang Parade shopping centre, this Uncle Lims has been around for many years. The food is average but I cant stop raving in about how they use REAL ginger in their teh halia  never seen
that before!!!

Selection of food is mainly loc dishes.

The store has plenty of seating with no need to wait. The space is clean and quite peaceful to sit and chill.

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Madam Kwan's NU Empire
collab_recs_with_scores.address: UG 09, Empire Shopping Gallery, Jalan SS 16/1, Ss 16, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: Adriel Wong
reviews.text: The food at Madam Kwan's is consistently good. You know what you're getting when you come here: classic Malaysian meals. While they are priced slightly higher, the portion sizes are quite big. Their fried rice dishes are recommended for me. However, just about anything you order will be, most likely, good. The restaurant is also nice as it's well-lit and very comfortable to have a nice quiet meal either on your own or with a group. Overall, I really recommend Madam Kwan's.

reviews.author: Badri Ibrahim
reviews.text: My familys all time favorite restaurant in Empire Subang. Delicious food serves ina great atmosphere. Kudos and big thank you to Mr Dharsen who has given his best service in attending us (dinner 25 April 2025). Keep up the good work!

reviews.author: maverick 17271
reviews.text: For the first time in a long time, this outlet has excellent service and food quality. Im glad we decided to give it another go after giving up on the place last year. Darshen was outstanding as he always is, but so were the other staff during a busy lunch hour today. Keep up the good work!

collab_recs_with_scores.name: BMS Organics @ NU Empire
collab_recs_with_scores.address: Lg-18, NU Empire, Jalan SS 16/1, Ss 16, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: Rabiatul Adawiyah
reviews.text: We enjoy BMS Nu Empire poke bowl. Just nice for a hearty meal before workout session. Thanks to Naja & Aini!

reviews.author: Vanessa Tham
reviews.text:
Staff Naja is very friendly, good service

The konjac drink is amazing

reviews.author: PUI LEONG KOK
reviews.text: Nu empire BMS organics store staff (Naja, Candy, Aini, Ang2) are warm n hospitable making each dining experience pleasant n enjoyable. They attend well to customers food requests n serve with a gracious attitude. Nice Team in the store!

collab_recs_with_scores.name: The Social @ Empire Subang
collab_recs_with_scores.address: Lot GK08 & GK08A, Ground Floor, Empire Shopping Gallery, Jalan SS16/1, Ss 16, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: minoo raj
reviews.text:
I loveee their food..this place is one I frequently
visit. Their devil we know pizza, lamb strips, Mediterranean salad with panner and the chicken 65 is amazing

As for the mantou bun with lamb.. the mantou bun is good but lamb doest taste good, smells and taste stale, the carbonara  pizza is okay okay.. but the garlic sause that comes with it is soo yummy.

Their cocktails are horrible unfortunately?, I  dislike the Moscow mule.. the pina colada tastes like antibiotics  but whiskey sour was okay.

As for the ambience.. love it, but the flush in the ladies isnt working.  The service is okay.

reviews.author: Arc To Arcturus
reviews.text: The margherita pizza wasn't actually Neapolitan but by Malaysian standards it was not bad. The Nepalese waiter was attentive but he had problems understanding our questions. Anyhow the food was good and worth coming back to try out the rest.

reviews.author: Shealy Sky
reviews.text: The atmosphere is so good especially during Christmas time. Recommended for gatherings with family or friends. They are offering lunch sets from Mondays to Fridays. The meal will come with small eats or a drink. Our fav is the Margarita Pizza. The Hainanese Chicken Chop and Maggie Mee Goreng are not bad too.

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Marutama Ramen
collab_recs_with_scores.address: LG-26, NU Empire Subang, Jalan SS 16/1, SS16, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: Sue Chuah
reviews.text:
Amazing Chicken Broth Ramen - Rich, Flavorful, and Unique!

Ive had countless bowls of ramen, but this was my first time trying one made with chicken broth-and wow, what a game-changer! The soup is thick, creamy, and packed with deep umami flavors, simmered for six hours daily. Its rich without being overly oily, and the best part? No preservatives or MSG!

The handmade ramen noodles are fresh, bouncy, and perfectly absorb the broth, making every bite flavorful.

The Ajitsuke Tamago Ramen comes with tender chicken chashu, seaweed, spring onions, and a beautifully seasoned egg-simple but absolutely satisfying. If you like a bit of spice, the Aka Charsiu Ramen is a must-try! The nutty, mildly spicy broth combined with lemon and pickled radish adds a refreshing twist.

They do have mini bowl size which is great for someone with petite appetite.

reviews.author: Allen Gong
reviews.text:
Marutama Ramen: A Quiet and Quality Ramen Experience

My recent visit to Marutama Ramen offered a peaceful and pleasant dining experience. While I personally am not a die-hard ramen enthusiast, I can certainly appreciate the quality of the food. The ramen was undeniably delicious, with a rich and flavorful broth and perfectly cooked noodles.

The staff were consistently attentive and friendly, ensuring that my dining experience was smooth and enjoyable. The restaurant itself boasted a nice and comfortable ambiance, creating a relaxing atmosphere for a meal. Notably, the restaurant was very quiet, which allowed for a more intimate and tranquil dining experience.

If you're a ramen lover seeking a quiet and comfortable environment to enjoy a quality bowl, Marutama Ramen is a great choice. While ramen may not be my personal favorite, I can definitely acknowledge the restaurant's commitment to providing a delicious and well-served meal.

Update 24 May 2025
Enthusiastic & Concise
Marutama Ramen delivers on all fronts, and their pan-fried dumplings are an absolute bomb! Seriously, these are a must-order. Each dumpling is perfectly crispy on the outside with a juicy, flavorful filling - pure perfection. As for the ramen, the broth is rich and comforting, and the noodles have that ideal springy texture. A fantastic spot for a satisfying meal.

reviews.author: Bchin Tan
reviews.text:
it was an incredible experience! The broth was rich and flavorful, perfectly balanced with the right amount of seasoning. The noodles had the perfect texture-chewy and fresh.

The atmosphere was cozy and welcoming, and the staff was friendly and attentive. Its great to find a place that serves authentic ramen

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Jibby & Co
collab_recs_with_scores.address: GK-11, Ground Floor, Empire Shopping Gallery, Jalan SS 16/1, Ss 16, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: Azhani Azizi
reviews.text:
The food was delicious - nothing mind blowing though for the price you pay for. It was delicious but for that price, you'd expect it to be a lot better. However, the food was beautifully plated and well balanced.

We booked a few days prior and we had no issues with our orders except for the fact that our meals were a little cold because they prepared it a little too early.

The atmosphere was nice with the decorations. Lunch hour was packed with office people, full house perhaps at one point. Parking was easy and the staffs were friendly.

reviews.author: Sean Tan
reviews.text: I quite liked the Thai Beef Ribs here. It was tender and delicious. But everything else wasn't nice, like the lamb shank, which was tender, but way too sweet. The eggs benedict thing was weird and not nice. The environment was one of the best for cafes, being quite modern and contemporary, with a touch of nature. The service was also not good, being less than minimal. The things here are way too expensive. The lamb shank was 85ringgit and wasn't even nice. Overall definitely not recommend.

reviews.author: Anne Sucila
reviews.text: The food was satisfactory as I had the tilapia fish and chips. Others with me had the breaded fried chicken, beef and grilled lamb. The person who had the beef said that it was good but the person who had the lamb said that she didn't like it. That's our honest opinion and review. I liked the greenery inside this place. But the place was a bit narrow as an old man knocked my elbow as he was going to the toilet and wasn't even aware that he did it. Unsure whether it was an accident but I chose to give him the benefit of the doubt.

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Eagle's Nest, KGNS
collab_recs_with_scores.address: Ss 11, 47301 Petaling Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: MFH - Malaysia Food Hunter
reviews.text:
Halal Chinese food. Only for club members.

Service is good, staffs are friendly. Place is clean, spacious and comfortable.

Food is so-so for me. Crispy fried chicken with crackers is kinda dry and over-fried, prawns were rather small and portions generally smaller than expected.

Bill came up to about RM150 - including rice and Chinese tea for 5 pax. All dishes were medium size and only omelette was small. Not too bad for the comfort of dining and service.

reviews.author: Ruben M
reviews.text: Really a good place for lunch or dinner. Food is reallly delicious, staff are friendly and ambiance is outstanding. Only if you order alcoholic drinks there would be a small issue. The foreign waiters are from the opposite bar and occasionally do not understand what we would want to order (we wanted to order campari and he stares at us blankly). Hope this improves.

reviews.author: Hussein Mohd Ariff
reviews.text: Ginger beef nice. Kailan garlic ok. Vege soup ok but a bit saulty. The rest so-so. Service was fast! Only 2 tables. Hmmm... maybe there is a correlation here.

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Restoran Kwai Sun
collab_recs_with_scores.address: No. 5G &7G, Jalan SS15/4G, Ss 15, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: tung how run
reviews.text: A true local delight! This is one of the most popular wanton noodle spots in the SS15 area. Dont miss the chance to try their classic black sauce wanton noodles-served with crispy pork lard, tender char siew, and green chilies. A nostalgic taste and definitely a must-try for noodle lovers!

reviews.author: ks
reviews.text: The food is great with many options available. There is kuih selling outside too. The drinks lady is also very hardworking and fast with handling many customers.

reviews.author: Kin Onn Chin
reviews.text: The pork noodles were quite good and affordable too. The popiah was lovely but need to sharpen knife to cut the popiah properly. The wanton noodles were tasty and generous, 7 wantons for rm9. The ice kopi O was most refreshing and nice. Lots of stalls to choose lots of variety. Free street parking available.

collab_recs_with_scores.name: KFC
collab_recs_with_scores.address: 17, Jln SS 15/4G, Ss 15, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia
collab_recs_with_scores.editorial_summary: Restaurant chain known for its buckets of fried chicken, plus combo meals & sides.

reviews.author: Chuah W Pang
reviews.text: Very clean and good air conditioning. Service a bit slow. Ran out of chicken at 6pm need to wait more than 10 minutes for new ones. Couldve prepared more for rush hour i guess. Anyway. Pretty good overall. Recommended KFC branch.

reviews.author: Jiawai Poh
reviews.text: The staffs are not focus in job , sit and chitchat at side . Nobody at counter to serve customer . The main point is I ordered family bucket with 15pcs and I only get 13pcs

reviews.author: Lyana Zuhaimi
reviews.text:
I ordered a chicken set from Grocery via Grab, but the nuggets (6 pieces) were missing! Nuggets were not included.
Please check again before giving it to Grab.

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Soi 55 Thai Kitchen  SS15 Subang Jaya
collab_recs_with_scores.address: 62, Jalan SS 15/4c, Ss 15, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: Khavinassh Mahendran
reviews.text:
If you're craving an authentic Thai feast in Subang Jaya, Soi 55 Thai Kitchen in SS15 is a must-visit! The cozy ambiance and friendly service make it a great spot for a casual meal, though be prepared for slightly higher prices.

I tried the Pineapple Fried Rice, which was fragrant and well-seasoned, with juicy pineapple chunks, cashews, and a hint of curry powder. The flavors were great, but for the price, the portion could have been more generous.

The Tom Yum Soup was packed with fresh seafood and had the perfect balance of spicy and sour flavors. It was definitely a highlight of the meal, but again, it felt a bit pricey for a simple dish.

For drinks, I had a Honey Lemon, which was refreshing, and they also offer a variety of soda drinks to cool down after the heat from the Tom Yum.

Overall, while the food at Soi 55 Thai Kitchen is delicious and authentic, the prices are on the higher side for basic Thai dishes. If you dont mind spending a little extra for quality flavors, its still worth a visit.

reviews.author: Marilyn
reviews.text:
Really enjoyed my time with my lovely teammates here! The food was nice and tasty, and its a great place to relax and enjoy a meal with friends or for a team lunch.

Cozy environment, friendly staff, and overall a very good experience.
Will definitely visit again!

reviews.author: Suhailah Mat Salim
reviews.text: Been here many times and the foods never disappoint and delicious. Can felt and taste the authentic foods from thai without need to go Thailand. The staff are friendly and the space comfortable and nice to hang out with friends.

reviews.author: Lisa Sabrina Gee
reviews.text:
Service was just okay. Went during weekend lunch. Not a lot of customers, but service and food took quite awhile to arrive. Wide range of menu varieties to choose from. Some of the dish portion is quite small for the price. Some of the price are also questionable like vegetables price are pricier than prawns dishes (?)

Overall dishes taste was good but couldve been better for the price range we are paying for. Nice ambiance for having family lunch/dinner.

reviews.author: Norsyahinda Atilah
reviews.text: we ordered nasi ayam kamheong and nasi kapitan fish fillet. both fulfilled our taste expectation. chicken were tender as they use boneless part. dumpling were good too only they put very less dipping soy sauce for the dish. drinks were good. the dining atmosphere was very calm and chill. also enjoyed free pudding from the restaurant. loved everything and definitely will repeat again to this restaurant

reviews.author: Nazliah Hamid
reviews.text: Food is great as always. Taste always the same. However, the chicken sweet and sour and the butter prawn, the portioning is always not consistent. In a month, my family and I come here at least 3 times a week. Please do better in your portioning. Tonight, the chicken pieces were small and the butter prawns were small too.

collab_recs_with_scores.name: Jyu Raku
collab_recs_with_scores.address: 13, Jalan SS 15/5a, Ss 15, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: Grace
reviews.text: First time visit this restaurant. The restaurant seems very popular and crowded. Better make a reservation before come. Food taste quite good, the sushi is fresh, the portion is generous yet the price is reasonable. Staff is attentive, fast serving and good service

reviews.author: Amy Chan
reviews.text: Great assortment of classical themed Japanese foods. The set meals were worth the money and the sashimi was of high quality. Would recommend the okonomiyaki as well as it is filled with octopus and seafood fillings.

reviews.author: Cheok Meng Law
reviews.text:
Food was alright but it didnt taste as good as it used to. Things like the side dishes were cut down a bit and the flavors werent amazing. The portions were good though but at this price point, we hoped itd taste better. One of the bentos were so delayed for some reason and only came by the time we were done with the other ones

The sukiyaki broth mostly tasted like soy sauce and didnt really have an onion or sweet umami flavor. The veggies were really filling though.

The fish was quite dry and relied on the sauce to be moist but even then it wasnt enjoyable.

The rolls and sashimi were alright.

collab_recs_with_scores.address: 7, Jalan SS 15/5a, Ss 15, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: Faisal Zubaid Akbar
reviews.text:
We have been to a few North Indian restaurants, and we always come back here.

The service, environment, and food are consistent and never disappoint.

They (the sfaff) always remember us every time we visit. Greet us warmly.

Keema matter, aloo gobi, naan, teh tarik are recommended.

reviews.author: Jeremie Ting
reviews.text: We returned after our inaugural visit two months ago, and with four of us, we ordered the Maharaja Khanna A Banquet that is suited for a minimum of four persons, which has a Starter, Main Course, and Dessert at RM218, exluding service tax. We took a snapshot of the menu and posted together with the dishes for reference. We added mango lasy, as well. Once again, the dishes did not disappoint and were tasty and nice, except for the yougurt dish, which is cold and we are not familiar with eating the yogurt with the rice and curry that tasted cold. We did not finish all the dishes and had to pack takeaway. The bill came to RM250.70  for the four of us.

reviews.author: Ginny Goh
reviews.text:
We have been coming to this DTandoor shop for 30+ years and the food/service has been consistently top notch!

One of the best Northern Indian food in Klang Valley! And most importantly, prices are reasonable!

collab_recs_with_scores.address: 5, Jalan SS 15/5a, Ss 15, 47500 Subang Jaya, Selangor, Malaysia

reviews.author: Jeremie Ting
reviews.text: An authentic Japanese restaurant with a Japanese owner. The atmosphere remains unchanged since our last visit some years ago. Parking was surprisingly available this time. The quality of food is still there and tasty, although the quantity is slightly reduced and the price higher. We ordered soft shell crab, saba fish, and three different beef sets. The bill came to RM264.75 for the three of us.

reviews.author: Khoo Yee Lun

The Yudofu is tasteless. The sento ika maruya is overcooked, very hard. Really disappointed  Won't visit again.

please dont waste your money and time here. Had the most terrible experience here ever.

to be honest, the price were decent but the quality and quantity of the food is totally not worth the price at all.

We orders a spider roll, description was stated as a soft shell crab roll. But it came out terrible. It was actually all just rice and there is NO soft shell crab meat at all. We then complained to the manager and the manager told us it meant to be that way??? We were stunned by her words totally not worth the price and time here at all. Very upsetting.

We even order a sushi and it look so terrible and not fresh. We dont even dare to eat it

Free flow ice cream was also a plus.

reviews.author: 4nsfr

Overall, it offers good food at a good value.

reviews.author: Eu Xin Hong
reviews.text:
Updated Review: 10th Nov 2024.

Staffs are super friendly and nice, comfy atmosphere. However, for the buffet it feels like some of the items wont be refilled overtime so it is recommended to arrive early. The food is subpar, meets the minimum requirements of a 2-3 star hotel standard.
Do not expect big meat items, if you're a sweet tooth then you might enjoy as i feel most of their pastry items are delicious!
However for the pricing, if you're paying full price then it might not really be worth it as it is a bit pricey, i used to get the discount rate on Eatigo (currently not available as of 15th Nov 2024).

>Breakfast buffet (N/P: RM25)
A decent selection of menu. Like the sausage and some stirfry, some items are a hit or miss.

>Lunch buffet (N/P: RM35)
A decent selection of local menu items. Pretty standard, i enjoy the pastry options.

>Dinner buffer (N/P: RM68)
N/A, but i attended the Diwali/Halloween 2024 special dinner buffet which has slightly more options than the usual, it is alright, some decent fish, prawn and meat items are provided as standard, soup is nice. And the pastry is overwhelming so its worth it if you're in it for the cakes and desserts.

>Afternoon tea set (RM25)
Its alright, a bit pricey, but pastries are good enough.

>Ala cart
It depends. Portion is generous

Lunch and Dinner buffet might not be conducted if theres not enough reservations so you need to call or Whatsapp them to confirm...

This place is more for the vibe, do not expect that the meat options can cover back your full price for the meal, the price is on the more expensive side. Their cakes is good though

reviews.author: Kai Xin
reviews.text:
had a try for their breakfast buffet, the normal price is 22, but I got the discount code on Eatigo so makes it 11 per pax so I'm much more happy & nothing to be picky

Buffet spread was not bad, had fried rice, fried egg & omelette, fried mee, sausages, baked potato, salad bar, basic nasi lemak with sotong sambal & ayam masak merah, plain porridge, a noodle section where you pick your own yellow mee/keoy teow and let them cook for you, soup of the day, toasted bread section, 3 fruits selection and cereals. Drinks included pink guava juice, apple juice, milk and coffee.

The sausage & cheese was quite salty, fried rice quite tasteless, but other then that most food is good! Instead of normal bread they do provide croissant and whole wheat bread as well.... And surprisingly the soup was Miso soup usually western buffet they'll have mushroom soup so I'm really shocked. Coffee machine is cool tho!

Overall not bad, love the relaxing vibes, service could be better,  will visit again.

P.S. most review on here was very bad so I had some preparation bfrhand but actually it wasn't that awful. Maybe just the dinner section was chaotic and everything messed up

reviews.author: MsOmNom
reviews.text:
Had a pleasant meal at Paradise Dynasty in Paradigm Mall recently. We arrived early at around 11.45am on a weekday so no crowds at that time. The standout for me was definitely their limited-time yellow wine xiao long bao - rich, flavorful, and something quite different from the usual offerings. The wonton soup also deserves a mention with its tasty broth, while the century egg with chili oil was nicely done and balanced.

On the other hand, the fried noodles and stir-fried cabbage were a bit too oily for my liking, which made those dishes less enjoyable.

The restaurant itself is nicely done up with a clean, modern interior. Service was generally good and attentive, though the staff did feel a little bit too eager when taking orders - we had to ask for more time before placing everything.

Overall though, it was a good experience and Id happily come back again to try more of their xiao long bao selections.

reviews.author: Bryan Lee
reviews.text:
Its been 4 years since I gave a review for this place and I thought of why not update since its been so long. And I did.

4 years since I reviewed this place and much have changed. The atmosphere for one is much improved, looks a lot more classy than before as reflective of the kind of restaurant it is, a classy and delicious Malay restaurant that serve above average quality food with above average price.

Speaking about price, it has increased way more than I anticipated! A classic Nasi Lemak Ayam Berempah is already above RM20, and its not close to Village Park Nasi Lemak. But even though there have been a couple changes for better or for worse, what doesnt change is how great and delicious their food is. Their Nasi Briyanj is still one of the best in Subang, and they still have one of the best Mee Bandung! Even my relatives from Muar were commending it to be better than its original recipe from Muar. But the price has also increased together with service charge & sst, one Mee Bandung cost RM20! 

Would I come regularly to pay this restaurant a visit? Certainly not, especially with that pricing. Once awhile still fine, to satisfy my desire of having a good Nasi Briyanj. But will definitely still recommend friends & family to this place & give a try cuz why not?!

reviews.author: Dennis Ng
reviews.text:
We placed our order for dine-in, but to our surprise, the entire restaurant was operating without air conditioning. When we asked the staff, they mentioned that it had been this way for several days.

It would have been much more considerate to place a notice at the entrance or inform customers before they order. We expected to enjoy our meal in a comfortable environment, but instead, we had to switch to takeaway after waiting, which was especially disappointing for the kids who were looking forward to dining in.

To make matters worse, the fries were served cold, with no warmth at all. This was really unacceptable, as we expected freshly prepared food.

On top of that, we were initially informed that the original fried chicken would take 15 minutes to be ready, which we were fine with. However, after waiting for 25 minutes, another staff member suddenly told us that we needed to wait another 15 minutes! I explained that we had already been waiting, and now it would take a total of 45 minutes-or possibly even longer? What is this, are they bringing the chicken fresh from the kampung?

With the whole environment already uncomfortably hot due to the lack of air conditioning, we ultimately decided to cancel the second order. This was truly a frustrating experience and an example of terrible customer service.

McDonald's, please do better!

reviews.author: SioChin Ang
reviews.text:
I had a wonderful dining experience at the outlet today, where a staff member with special needs provided incredibly thoughtful service. He noticed me struggling to wash my hands as the water tap only dispensed water for two seconds at a time. Without hesitation, he held the tap for me, until i was done washing. It was a small but meaningful gesture that made my visit even more memorable.
I caught a glimpse of his name tag (his name is Hanif) and thanked him for that with much appreciation.
Having said that, management should look into changing the tap for patron's convenience.

reviews.author: Edmund JP
reviews.text:
Restaurant Gold Dragon City is an old-school Chinese restaurant that has stood the test of time. The food is delicious, with classic flavors that remind you of traditional Chinese banquets. Their dishes are well-prepared, flavorful, and perfect for sharing with family and friends. Service is good, with attentive staff who ensure that orders are served efficiently.

The atmosphere has an old-time charm, and while it may not be the trendiest spot, it's always popular with regulars who appreciate its consistency. However, parking can be quite challenging, especially during peak hours, so be prepared to circle around or park a little further away. Despite this, the quality of the food makes it worth the effort. If you enjoy classic Chinese cuisine in a nostalgic setting, this is a great place to visit!

reviews.author: Ching Yieng Wong
reviews.text: Really surprised by this unassuming restaurant in PJ which has been there for ages. Tested by time, good taste and thumbs up for the signature dishes. The deboned village chicken was tender and tasty, yam pork ribs and fried brinjal were all good. Steamed garoupa was ok and other dishes were also good. Special call out to the signature tofu which was soft and delicious.

reviews.author: lil tweet
reviews.text:
My goodness. I know this is a restaurant of great reputation. Everything didn't go well for my visit here on a Friday night.

The restaurant was about 60% occupied but the service was bad due to lack of staff. We had pre-ordered but no one came to confirm anything with us, even though we were shown to our Reserved tables.

The food was absolutely lousy.  I rarely give less than 3 stars but this time, I have to do what I need to do. Perhaps our mistake was we ordered from the set menu. The crispy chicken with Thai sauce is the most stale fried chicken I've encountered. The chicken smells of cupboard or plastic container. I understand they need to pre-cook and put aside. But this tasted like days old!!

Nothing was appetising even though Set A dishes were popular dishes.

And I know this is an old restaurant. But the musky odor at the staircase is really quite a turn-off.

reviews.author: Pam Ko Fit Foodie
reviews.text:
My English Korean family loves padang style food so much. So today after being a regular to sederhana masakan, we wanted to try sari ratu prima. We arrived around 1 PM, which considered not an ideal lunch time for enjoying freshly cook piping hot dishes, though we decided to go since we woke up late.

The dishes were mostly gone and some left after gathered into pile of small plates once they transferred to from the rectangular tray.
We chose our all time padang dishes like dengdeng belado, rendang daging, daun ubi(singkong?), chengkol, sambal petai udang, nanka gulai with 2 rice.

But the rice seems expensive and very small in portion, so we had asked one extra. But I saw some other customers were having a massive portion of individual plates even though it's not nasi campur setting!

Anyway we also orders 2 ice water and one coffee O kosong panas for me as I needed some caffeine to wake myself fully up.
The dishes we chose were all delicious and lovely, but due to the fact we went quite later lunch time, the food wasn't that warm which is one downside,  but we won't repeat to come late next time anyway.

Lets talk about some our favorite padang dishes! Here, the dengdeng belado topped with red chiili is just ok, thanks to freshly made red sambal, but the beef itself wasn't that amazing unlike that of Sederhana we enjoyed. It is bcoz the beef here was way way too thin and deep oil fried I can tell, the texture was like some sort of chips in a hint of meat form. It should be more chewy and meatier jerky! Here it's just flaky chips or keropok like texture in oily form.
Please better improve the thickness of the dengdeng, should be more thicker so that customers can savor the beefy flavors while biting and chewing, not feathery ultra light crisps! 

The staff who was explaining the price one by one was very friendly and attentive, except the one upsetting price dispute for sambal prawn petai upon paying the bill lastly.
That said, undeniably its the most surprising cons to us after the meal, that the price we were told was rm8.50 for sambal udang petai when I asked pointing it with my hand last minute, so I said satu pinggan. But it turned out to be rm 16.25, almost double price just for 5 prawns. It's ok 5 prawns for rm8.50,  not for rm16.25. Basically the each prawn costs over rm3 each which seemed very very expensive. Since I live here, when I can buy fresh prawns per kilo for rm 40 easily, and i usually get 20 fresh prawns for just half kilo(rm20)
So i told that staff who also was in charge of the payment counter, but he said it's the price, but they only gave us 5 prawns for such high price? If we knew, we could have ordered another plate for daging rendang something.
Other than these two dishes. All is amazing and tasty! 

Just white rice potion seems too little for rm2.25, which I couldn't get rid of in my mind until the next meal! , The totall bill after 6% tax added came around rm70.

Interior is simple, yet clean and morden with a nice minang traditional house painting on the wall! Strong aircon and clean washroom too!

Despite of the price dispute for the sambal prawn udang petai, we likely come back soon to try more other yummy looking dishes at the very right time!

reviews.author: tia idrus
reviews.text:
We used to frequent this place when they first opened here. Glad to see that it's still operating and still has many patrons. Food is still good but the selection is the same. If you crave for nasi padang style dishes, this is the place to be.

We were satisfied with the food we had, we ordered our usual selection of dishes: Ikan Nila Terbang, Ayam Goreng, Ikan Keli, sambal ijo and many others. Our favorite is the Ikan Nila terbang which is served together with the black sauce, so good especially if they fry it fresh for you.

Price is on the higher side, definitely more expensive than your regular economy rice but pretty standard for nasi padang dishes. We ordered 8 types of lauk with 4 plates of rice and they set us back about RM200. Of course this varies according to the types of lauk you choose.

Recommended if you're looking for Indonesian-style rice and if you're hungry because most of the lauk are already ready so they'll serve you promptly. Only thing I don't like here is the parking, however, the restaurant does have limited reserved spots for patrons for their convenience.

reviews.author: Veeraphandian Jagaveran
reviews.text:
Visited Wong Kok recently with high expectations, especially since the ambience was comfortable and the service was generally polite. The menu offers a wide variety of Hong Kong-style dishes, and the environment is suitable for casual dining.

However, I couldnt help but feel that some of the food lacked freshness. A few items, particularly the breakfast meats, tasted like they were pre-cooked or frozen, then simply reheated. The texture and flavor just didnt deliver the freshness or depth expected from a dine-in restaurant.

That said, not all dishes were disappointing - some items were tasty and served warm with good presentation. Theres definitely potential, but consistency and food quality need improvement if Wong Kok wants to stand out.

reviews.author: Jackie
reviews.text:
WongKok used to have a branch in Johor Bahru, but as with all businesses who tried expanding southwards, there's this thing about unpredictability of patrons in Johor state, particularly if you are targeting Singaporeans. One moment they "flood" an outlet, the next few months, boredom sets in and they frequent the place lesser and lesser. This happened with Jordan HongKong restaurant and KimGary, they are both struggling to survive monthly.
Compared to Jordan and KimGary,
1. The prices are slightly more affordable.
2. The portions are generous
3. The taste is more delectable and memorable.
We ordered the spaghetti with Cheese and a Pork Chop Western serve. Both came out outstanding! Better than the rest. Their Menu is not extensive, which is fine because better to concentrate on what you are good at rather thsn trying to serve everyone and everything at the same time!
